objective_observations.description: CORRECTION to OBS-ASHFORDINN-001: tile IDs on Inside_B.png are addressed by rmmz_core.js's real formula (sx_col=(tileId//128)%2*8+tileId%8, sy_row=(tileId%256)//8%16), not a naive row-major 16-per-row grid. Re-verifying the original (pre-rebuild) Map026 furniture by rendering each tile with the real formula and cropping precisely (not eyeballing a hand-derived grid) shows: tile IDs 88-92/104-108 (the 'three mismatched bed styles' claim) are actually five pieces of one connected red sofa/couch object (corner, straight, straight, straight, corner), placed at three disconnected map locations with gaps between them, not three different bed graphics. Tile IDs 24-28 (the 'floor-placed picture frames' claim) are pillars, a crate, and rock-pile graphics, not picture frames. Tile IDs 126/127/142/143 (the 'incomplete fireplace missing a pillar' claim) are a wall mirror and a blank frame over a shelf with bowls, not any part of a fireplace -- no multi-column fireplace-pediment object exists on this sheet at all; a complete single-tile fireplace graphic exists elsewhere (tile 195).

value.corrects: OBS-ASHFORDINN-001 structural_object findings for fireplace, three bed variants, and picture frames
value.root_cause: OBS-ASHFORDINN-001's tile identification appears to have assumed row-major sheet addressing rather than rmmz_core.js's actual formula; this session's own first rebuild attempt (now superseded) made the identical mistake and was only caught by rendering the result and finding it did not match intent, per academy/knowledge/classic-jrpg-feel.md's render-first discipline.

objective_observations.description: The original 2026-07-08 human rejection reasons ('rocks appear in the room', 'sofa/furniture rendering is poor') are both confirmed accurate by this corrected re-verification. OBS-ASHFORDINN-001's conclusion that the rock claim was 'not corroborated' was itself the error, not the original human review.

Rebuild applied: a complete single-tile fireplace (tile 195) placed at (8,3), the same tile as the pre-existing !Flame hearth event, so the flame animation now renders over an actual fireplace graphic. A picture-and-cabinet nook (landscape/portrait paintings, tiles 130/131/133, over matching dresser/cabinet furniture, tiles 136/138/140) at (3-5, 2-3). Two consistent bed alcoves (tile 168 top / 176 bottom, the same style twice) at (11,4-5) and (13,4-5), replacing the scattered sofa pieces. Border tileId-1536 (Inside_A5 kind0, confirmed solid black by direct pixel sampling, same bug class found repeatedly elsewhere this session) replaced with this file's own already-in-use wall-body tile (7048).

subjective_observations.description: The rebuilt room now reads with a single legible dominant landmark (the fireplace, centered, with the flame event correctly seated on it), a distinct secondary furnished corner (pictures over dresser), and two clearly separated bed alcoves -- a meaningful improvement against academy/knowledge/classic-jrpg-feel.md's Review Gate over the pre-rebuild state, where furniture competed for attention without any of it being correctly assembled in the first place.

notes: Supersedes OBS-ASHFORDINN-001's structural_object tile-identification findings; does not dispute its dimensions/event/border findings, which remain accurate. Produced during a direct rebuild pass, not a pure observation pass -- filed after the fact to correct the record given the significance of the tile-identification error and its propagation into GRD-ASHFORDINN-002/003.
